commit.message:
Add custom Dockerfile to fix sqlite3 native binding compilation

The n8n-nodes-sqlite community package requires native Node.js bindings
(sqlite3) that must be compiled from source. The base N8N image (Alpine)
lacks the necessary build tools (python3, make, g++).

Changes:
- Add infra/Dockerfile: extends n8nio/n8n with build tools
- Update infra/docker-compose.yml: use build: instead of image:,
  tag local image as botania/n8n:local
- Update docs/setup/02-n8n.md: add Dockerfile step, build command,
  SQLite community node installation instructions

To apply in the VM:
  cp ~/botania-repo/infra/Dockerfile ~/botania/
  cp ~/botania-repo/infra/docker-compose.yml ~/botania/
  cd ~/botania && docker compose build && docker compose up -d

commit.message:
Replace volatility with Realized Volatility from intraday data

- Create data_pipeline/moex_intraday.py: fetches 10-minute candles
  for IMOEX from MOEX ISS API, computes daily RV
- RV formula: RV_t = sqrt(sum(r²_{t,i})) * sqrt(252) * 100
  where r_{t,i} are intraday log returns between consecutive candles
- Fetched 110,993 intraday candles (2018-01-03 to 2026-03-06)
- Computed RV for 2,053 trading days (mean 14.6%, peak 510% on
  2022-02-24 — Ukraine invasion shock)
- Pipeline step 7 now fetches intraday candles month-by-month
  with pagination before computing daily factors
- Updated volatility factor description in sidebar

commit.message:
v2.2.0: NoizDNS stealth mode, EDNS probing, NXDOMAIN detection

Scanner improvements:
- EDNS size probing: test 512, 900, 1232 separately, report max
- NXDOMAIN hijacking detection: query .invalid, check for RCODE=3
- Score now 6-point (added EDNS size + NXDOMAIN tests)
- Score filter options extended to 5+

NoizDNS:
- Add stealth mode toggle (slower but harder to detect by DPI)
- Stealth uses faster cover traffic and moderate KCP settings
- Database migration v19→v20 for noizdns_stealth column

Build:
- Bump version to 2.2.0 (code 35)
- Lite flavor: arm64-only (ndk.abiFilters) to reduce APK size
- CI: collect only useful APKs (skip lite per-ABI splits)
- Remove duplicate IPs from resolver list (90 removed)
- gomobile build uses noizdns/mobile (private) for both flavors

Flatten navigation to Races | Training | Toolkit tab bar

All tools, crew access, and settings consolidated into Toolkit tab.
Crew detection now per-race via importedProfiles instead of global appMode.
Onboarding simplified: no role selection, everyone starts as runner.
Also includes training view review (sheet-based planners, ETA refactor)
and UX research docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: await tech stack refresh before returning response (Vercel kill bug)

Root cause: Phase 3 (news) and Phase 4 (tech stacks) were
fire-and-forget promises. Vercel terminates serverless functions
immediately after the HTTP response is sent, so these promises
were killed mid-execution and never wrote to the DB.

Fixes:
1. CRITICAL: Await refreshTechStacksForCompanies and
   refreshNewsCacheForActiveCompanies before returning the response.
   The 300s maxDuration gives plenty of headroom.
2. CRITICAL: Filter out empty JSONB arrays ('[]') in the aggregation
   query — the tech_stack column defaults to '[]', which passes
   through a NOT NULL filter but contains no data.
3. Defensive: Null-safe comparison on new_jobs column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(eczema): redesign zone scoring panel with plain-language parameters

Replace opaque clinical jargon (Erythema, Papulation, Excoriation,
Lichenification) with 7 user-friendly skin descriptors, each shown
with an emoji icon, plain question, and a 4-dot visual severity selector
(None / Mild / Moderate / Severe) instead of ChoiceChips.

EASI score is still calculated from the original 4 core parameters
(backward compatible). New supplementary parameters are stored in the
existing affected_areas JSON blob — no backend migration required.

Zone-level parameters (7 total):
- Redness           🔴  Erythema         [EASI core]
- Bumps & Swelling  🫧  Papulation        [EASI core]
- Scratch Marks     🩹  Excoriation       [EASI core]
- Skin Thickening   🪨  Lichenification   [EASI core] — desc updated to
                         include "sandpaper-like texture"
- Weeping/Crusting  💧  Oozing (SCORAD)  [supplementary]
- Dryness/Flaking   🌵  Xerosis (POEM)   [supplementary]
- Skin Darkening    🌑  PIH              [supplementary — captures
                         post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, esp. relevant
                         for darker skin tones where this is often the most
                         distressing long-term sequela]

Area selector redesigned: 6 numbered tiles (green→red gradient) with
friendly labels (Tiny / Small / Some / Large / Mostly / All) + % note.

Per-visit: renamed "Pruritus (Itch) VAS" → "Itch Intensity".
Instruction hint: "Tap a body zone to describe how the skin looks there".

commit.message:
fix: couple live timer to test lifecycle, fix status badge, preserve batch

Issue 1 — Timer locked to test lifecycle:
- timer_management.py: start_live_timer now returns 400 if test.status != 'published'.
  Timer can only be started after admin clicks Start Test.
- clap_test_assignment.py: unassign_clap_test now clears global_deadline and
  timer_extension_log atomically when the test is stopped.
- tests/page.tsx: Start Live Timer button disabled + amber warning shown when
  test is not yet Published; title tooltip explains the required action.
- tests/page.tsx: handleUnassignClapTest clears liveTimer/liveTimerTimeLeft state
  immediately so the UI reflects the cleared timer without a page refresh.

Issue 2 — Status badge updates on-the-spot:
- handleAssignClapTest: optimistic update now includes status:'published' so
  the Draft → Published badge switches instantly without waiting for fetchClapTests().
- handleUnassignClapTest: optimistic update includes status:'draft'.
- All rollback paths restore both is_assigned and status together.

Issue 3 — Batch allotment preserved across Start/Stop:
- clap_test_assignment.py assign: batch_id is NEVER changed here; backend reads
  the test's existing batch_id (set once via Edit tab). Returns 400 if no batch
  is configured. Removed batch_id from request body and .update() call entirely.
- clap_test_assignment.py unassign: removed batch_id=None; only status, timer
  fields and StudentClapAssignment rows change. Batch FK is preserved.
- tests/page.tsx: removed batch_id from assign request body; optimistic updates
  leave batch_id/batch_name untouched.

commit.message:
Fix 3 bugs from round 7 audit, add 5 regression tests

Bug fixes:
- Bootstrap: cluster weights by firm_symbol instead of event_id.
  The wild bootstrap must assign the same weight to all events from
  the same firm to preserve cross-sectional dependence. Using
  event_id gave each event independent weights, violating the
  theoretical guarantees of the method.
- VolatilityModel: validate estimation window variance > 0 before
  computing r^2/var. Zero variance (constant returns) caused
  Inf/NaN residuals and downstream failures.
- RollingWindowModel: validate effective window size >= 3 before
  computing sigma = sqrt(SS/(ws-2)). ws=2 caused division by zero.

Test suite: 832 passing, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  OracleConnectorPX), daarna SubRecord als fallback (CustomStage). Eerder

commit.message:
Optimize throughput: 3.6-5.4x speedup via selective models and batched inference (#3)

* Optimize face recognition throughput with model-selective pipelines and batched inference

Bypass InsightFace's app.get() which runs all 5 models per face regardless of endpoint.
Each endpoint now runs only the models it needs: detect() skips 4 models, embed() skips
landmarks and genderage, analyze() skips landmarks. Recognition is batched via get_feat()
across all faces within and across images. Batch endpoints use cross-image batching with
a single semaphore acquisition.

RTX 4090 results: detect 78ms→14.5ms (5.4x), embed 78ms→21.5ms (3.6x),
embed_batch(8) 616ms→157ms (3.9x). Embeddings verified identical (cosine sim 1.0).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Convert to single databricks bundle deploy with comprehensive docs and diagrams

Replace the monolithic 544-line databricks.yml with a modular bundle structure:
- databricks.yml: variables + includes + targets only
- resources/app.yml: Databricks App with SQL Warehouse + Lakebase resources
- resources/jobs.yml: 10 serverless jobs (all environment_key, no new_cluster)
- resources/pipelines.yml: 4 serverless DLT pipelines (no cluster blocks)
- resources/model_serving.yml: 2 model serving endpoints
- resources/experiments.yml: 5 MLflow experiments

Remove all hardcoded values:
- shared.py: _CATALOG from DATABRICKS_CATALOG env var
- genie.py: workspace URL from DATABRICKS_HOST env var
- copilot.py: VS endpoint/index from env vars
- 6 pipeline notebooks: catalog via dbutils.widgets.get("catalog")
- 2 Lakebase notebooks: parameterized SP UUID, instance, database

Add deployment tooling:
- post_deploy.sh: Lakebase perms, Synced Tables, Genie Spaces
- deploy.sh: deprecation notice (FORCE_LEGACY_DEPLOY=1 to bypass)
- job_00_setup: one-time schemas + tables + backfill
- job_09_simulator: manual start, continuous
- job_10_dashboard_snapshots: every 5 min

Update documentation:
- README.md: complete rewrite with bundle deploy instructions, architecture
  diagrams, data serving layers, AI Copilot tools, Genie spaces
- docs/DEPLOYMENT.md: comprehensive step-by-step bundle deploy guide
- CHANGELOG.md: detailed change entry
- 3 architecture diagrams (deployment flow, bundle architecture, data
  serving layers) as PNG + Mermaid source
